task_completion.description: Parent collection 'Fourth Amendment Research' and subcollections 'Favorable Precedent' and 'Adverse Precedent' are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left pane of Juris-M, a folder named 'Fourth Amendment Research' is visible, containing subfolders 'Favorable Precedent' and 'Adverse Precedent'.

task_completion.description: At least 6 Fourth Amendment SCOTUS cases are added, including Terry v. Ohio, Katz v. United States, and Carpenter v. United States.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the middle pane (item list), there are at least 6 case items. Terry v. Ohio, Katz v. United States, and Carpenter v. United States must be visible among them.

task_completion.description: Cases are assigned to the appropriate subcollections based on their legal holding.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show cases being placed into or viewed within the 'Favorable Precedent' and 'Adverse Precedent' subcollections. Favorable should include cases like Katz and Carpenter; Adverse should include cases like Terry.

task_completion.description: Each case has a note explaining its key holding (minimum 40 words).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Note icons are visible as child items under the cases in the middle pane, or the right pane displays a note with a substantial paragraph of text (at least ~40 words) for the selected case.

task_completion.description: The tag 'fourth-amendment' is applied to the cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'fourth-amendment' is visible in the tag selector (bottom left pane) or in the 'Tags' tab in the right pane for the added cases.

task_completion.description: Pre-existing items Marbury v. Madison and Brown v. Board of Education are not modified or deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Marbury v. Madison and Brown v. Board of Education are still visible in the 'My Library' view and do not have the 'fourth-amendment' tag or new notes attached.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Juris-M GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Juris-M application open with menus, dialogs, or text fields being actively used, rather than terminal commands modifying the database.

integrity.description: The agent used real legal data for the cases and holdings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The case titles, citations, and note contents reflect actual legal information, not placeholder text like 'test case' or 'lorem ipsum'.

integrity.description: The agent did not alter the pre-existing cases to fulfill the 6-case requirement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Marbury v. Madison and Brown v. Board of Education remain in their original state without the 'fourth-amendment' tag or new notes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Required cases: Terry v. Ohio (392 U.S. 1), Katz v. United States (389 U.S. 347), Carpenter v. United States (585 U.S. 296). Favorable precedent (pro-defense/privacy) includes Katz, Carpenter, Kyllo, Riley. Adverse precedent (pro-government/police) includes Terry, Jones, Gates. Pre-existing items to remain untouched: Marbury v. Madison, Brown v. Board of Education. The tag to be used is exactly 'fourth-amendment'.
